assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: The response clearly defines 'Available' as 'The version is officially available in your release channel. You can manually upgrade to it right now if you want.'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: The response defines 'Default' as 'The version used for new cluster creation' and explains they can differ during rollouts, though it doesn't specifically mention minor version introduction stages

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: The response defines 'Auto-upgrade target' as 'The version GKE will actually upgrade your existing cluster to automatically' and mentions it's cluster-specific and depends on maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: The response explains these can differ and provides a specific example where default (1.31.8) differs from auto-upgrade target (1.31.7), though it focuses more on patch versions than minor version rollouts specifically

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: The response mentions that auto-upgrade target is cluster-specific and depends on maintenance exclusions, though it doesn't explicitly state that different clusters on the same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: The response specifically mentions 'if you had a "no minor upgrades" exclusion, your target might be limited to patches within your current minor version only'

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: The response provides the exact command 'gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION' to check auto-upgrade target, though it doesn't specifically mention EoS timestamps

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: The response does not mention GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
